page: Microsoft Azure HC44rs is an HCS family server instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8168 processor. It features 44 dedicated physical x86_64 cores with a base clock speed of 2.7 GHz and a single thread per core configuration. The system is equipped with 352.0 GB of RAM, providing a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It includes 717 GB of local storage, supports Premium Storage, and is RDMA capable, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core processing capabilities, with stress-ng multi-core CPU performance, bzip3 multi-threaded decompression, and Passmark CPU scores all placing in the top 25%. Memory performance is also notable, with read-only memory bandwidth for both small and large blocks ranking in the top 25%. However, single-threaded performance is more moderate, as shown by average single-core stress-ng and Geekbench scores, alongside a weak rating for single-threaded gzip compression.

From a cost efficiency perspective, the HC44rs offers high resource density with dedicated physical cores and a substantial memory footprint, making it a viable option for memory-intensive workloads without the overhead of hyperthreading. It demonstrates strong performance in database operations and Redis SET operations, both scoring in the top 25%. This makes the instance highly suitable for relational databases, in-memory caching, software compilation, and high-performance computing tasks requiring RDMA networking.
description: Microsoft Azure HC44rs is an HCS family server instance designed for high-performance computing and memory-intensive workloads. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8168 processor with 44 dedicated physical x86_64 cores running at 2.7 GHz, it features 352.0 GB of RAM and 717 GB of local storage. The instance is RDMA and Premium Storage capable, operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. Benchmarks show strong multi-core CPU performance, high memory bandwidth, and excellent database operation throughput, all ranking in the top 25%. Conversely, single-threaded performance is average, with weak single-threaded gzip compression. Given its high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core and dedicated CPU allocation, the HC44rs is well-suited for database management, software compilation, in-memory caching, and parallel processing workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure HC44rs is an HCS family server featuring 44 dedicated Intel Xeon 8168 cores, 352 GB of RAM, and 717 GB of local storage, optimized for memory-intensive and RDMA-enabled workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure HC44rs is an HCS family server with 44 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 352 GB RAM, and RDMA support for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A dedicated multi-core server featuring high memory bandwidth, RDMA capability, and substantial RAM for demanding database and parallel computing workloads.
bullet_points: 44 dedicated physical Intel Xeon 8168 cores with no hyperthreading
352 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
717 GB of local storage with Premium Storage and RDMA support
Strong multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance
Optimized for database operations, software compilation, and in-memory caching
